calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-30-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mercy Hall for Charles Tucker and Sheila Bradford, Co-Trustees (via Zoom).

The Court inquired whether Ms. Hall had reviewed the tentative ruling. Ms. Hall confirmed review and raised a question concerning attorney's fees incurred for filing a petition for instructions regarding reimbursement of a Medi-Cal claim. Ms. Hall argued that due to conflicting appellate decisions regarding Medi-Cal estate recovery, the petition for instructions was filed in order to resolve the ambiguity and fulfill fiduciary duties.

The Court noted that Judge Castellanos had previously referred to the petition as frivolous and indicated an intent to reduce the requested fees accordingly. Ms. Hall urged the Court to reconsider, emphasizing the need for judicial guidance in light of conflicting appellate authority. The Court agreed to re-examine the issue and consider whether such a conflict exists when reviewing the fee request.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant the petition, subject to review of the fee issue.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-11-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall appeared by zoom for Charles Tucker (petitioner).
Emnanuelle Soichet for The Attorney General.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the issue of whether it is appropriate for the Special Needs Trust to reimburse Medi-Cal. Discussion was also held regarding language in the trust instrument and Article 5 section F 1. b. Upon conclusion of argument, the court stated that it totally agrees with the States position, denied the Petition for Instructions and orders reimbursement to Medi-Cal for the full amount to the penny. Mrs. Soichet is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Alex Chen via Zoom videoconference for Petitioner.

The Court sets forth the petition before the court and stated there was a notice problem. Mr. Chen stated there is no prejudice with notice, that the petitioner is the trustee, that all parties know each other, and it was served for the proper date. The Court noted the inconsistency of the notice, waives that defect; and enquires about the verification problem on the amendment. Mr. Chen provides explanation and stated the verification has been corrected. The Court also waives those defects on counsel's representations. Mr. Chen presents oral argument regarding fees and costs: 2018 attorney fees not addressed in prior accounting, there was one court call appearance not needed and he got a refund of $94.00 from court call, that he can verify each of the other charges for file and serve express and filing fees. The Court noted those fees were not submitted in writing and they do not sync up with the register of actions. Mr. Chen stated he would be happy to submit a supplemental filing. The Court stated the time has passed for supplemental filings as counsel did not ask for more time, that these fees did not come up before and the hearing is now. Mr. Chen stated his office needs to recover those fees, that his boss usually charges $400.00/hr but charging $250.00 and his normal rate is $325.00 but charging reducing to $275.00 this matter. The Court noted counsel is seeking fees outside of current accounting period and things that needed to be corrected were simple. Mr. Chen stated he had to submit 2 different declarations to explain one message to get across. The Petition for Approval of Fourth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alex Chen for Zions Trust (Petitioner).

Mr. Chen addressed the issue of notice to Jasmine stating that she is a Minor with a disability and has no way of comprehending, and that the Father who is also the Guardian Ad Litem was noticed. Mr. Chen also stated that medically Jasmine is unable to live at home, that she is in Saratoga, that the parents are in San Francisco, that the travel expense reimbursement amount was agreed upon with the Parents and the Guardian about five or six years ago, and he believes that was the rate set by the IRS at that time. The Court noted that the rate set by the IRS for medical travel is $.17 and $.535 for business travel. Mr. Chen stated that he believes the agreed rate may have been based on the business travel rate, that the Parents visit Jasmine several times a week, that it is a benefit to Jasmine, that they would otherwise not be able to visit as often and his position is to keep that rate. In addition, Mr. Chen stated that the $40.00 a month is for other essential items such as clothing, celebration of and birthday gifts, that most of the costs for clothing comes from the Parents, that the 1% Trustee fee agreement was agreed upon by the Guardian, that ETF's are a benefit to the Trust because they cover a broad share of the market and they are diversified at a lower risk. Furthermore, Mr. Chen stated that the Trustee pays for the Minor to live at the hospital, tax expenses, travel reimbursement expenses, caregiving costs and some clothing, that he has no other suggestions/rates in mind, and that he agrees to a reduction in fees in the amount $110.00 for .4 hours in Attorney time and $135.00 for one hour of Paralegal time (total reduction of $235.00/total attorney fees of $3,397.50). The Court approves waiver of notice to Jasmine. The Court informed Counsel that in the future it will require authorization for the purchase of ETF's, that it will not approve any purchases made without prior Court approval, that the Trustee is required to keep time sheets and noted that the 1% Trustee fee was not part of the Court order establishing the Special Needs Trust. The Petition for Approval of Third Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Traci Regli appeared by telephone for Western National Trust Company.

Ms. Regli stated that they are having difficulty finding someone who is an expert to assist with regard what other services could be provided to benefit Jasmine. Status on filing of reports from primary care and educational providers was continued to November 10, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

First Account & Rept. Of Trustee, Petn. For Fees & Attorneys Compensation
Off Calendar Feb-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Tracy Regli for Western National Trust.

Ms. Regli addressed issues regarding the investments, Jasmine's medical/physical condition, Jasmine's needs being provided by the care facility and the regional center, whether any other services could be provided to enhance Jasmine's quality of life, the matter being categorized incorrectly by the bank and falling through the cracks, fees, how fees were calculated, etc. The accounting petition was taken under submission. Ms. Regli is to obtain reports from the primary care provider and the educational providers as to what other services could be provided for the benefit of Jasmine. Status on filing of said reports was set for April 23, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
